inference_unit.notations: - $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature - Hg-1201: The chemical compound $HgBa_{2}CuO_{4+\delta}$ - Tl-1201: The chemical compound $TlBa_{2}CuO_{5}$ - Tl-2223: The chemical compound $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{10}$ - Hg-1212: The chemical compound $HgBa_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{6+x}$ (or $HgBa_{2}RCu_{2}O_{6+x}$ where $R$ is a rare-earth element or Calcium) - Hg-1223: The chemical compound $HgBa_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{8+x}$ - $n$: The number of $CuO_{2}$ layers per unit cell in homologous series of copper-oxide superconductors - $\rho$: Mass density - $\delta, x$: Non-stoichiometric oxygen content - $R$: A rare-earth element - HBCCO: The mercury-barium-calcium-copper-oxide system

premises.premise: The copper-oxide compound $HgBa_{2}CuO_{4+\delta}$ (referred to as Hg-1201) exhibits superconductivity characterized by a transition temperature $T_{c}$ of 94 K. [1]

premise.title: Structural analogy with Tl-based copper oxides

premises.premise: The crystal structure of $HgBa_{2}CuO_{4+\delta}$ (Hg-1201) is similar to that of the thallium-based copper oxide $TlBa_{2}CuO_{5}$ (Tl-1201), which suggests that mercury-based copper oxides form a homologous series with the general chemical formula $HgBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+2+\delta}$, where $n$ is the number of $CuO_{2}$ layers. [1]

In the thallium-based superconducting homologous series defined by the formula $TlBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+3}$, the superconducting transition temperatures $T_{c}$ vary with the number of $CuO_{2}$ layers $n$, ranging from $T_{c} < 10$ K for $n=1$ to approximately $T_{c} \approx 110$ K for $n=3$. [4, 5]

The established record for the highest superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ prior to the study of the $HgBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+2+\delta}$ system was 125–127 K, observed in the thallium-based compound $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{10}$ (Tl-2223). [2, 3]

premise.title: Absence of superconductivity in Hg-1212 rare-earth variants

premises.premise: In the compound $HgBa_{2}RCu_{2}O_{6+x}$ (referred to as Hg-1212), where $R$ represents a rare-earth element such as Europium (Eu) or Calcium (Ca), experimental synthesis has been reported but no superconducting transition was detected in that specific material system. [6]
premises.assumption: For the purpose of calculating the superconducting volume fraction from experimental magnetic susceptibility measurements in the Hg-Ba-Ca-Cu-O (HBCCO) system, the average mass density of the sample is assumed to be $\rho \approx 6 \text{ g cm}^{-3}$.
